bands.label: low
bands.text:
Hands-on, physical work with tools or machinery holds little appeal for you. You prefer tasks that don't involve working with your hands or with equipment.

This isn't a shortcoming — your professional energy is simply directed elsewhere, where the hands-on component is secondary.

bands.label: average
bands.text:
You're capable of handling practical, technical tasks when necessary, though they're not your main professional passion.

This gives you flexibility in roles that combine hands-on and non-hands-on components.

bands.label: high
bands.text:
You enjoy working with your hands, tools, machinery, or in a physical space. A concrete, tangible result matters more to you than abstract theory.

This is a valuable trait for technical, engineering, and craft professions where hands-on skill is essential.

questions.text: I enjoy working with my hands using tools or equipment.
I prefer practical tasks over theoretical ones.
I'm curious about how machines and mechanisms work.
I get satisfaction from physical work.

bands.label: low
bands.text:
Analytical research and solving complex, abstract problems aren't things you're naturally drawn to.

You're likely more effective in roles built around action or interaction rather than prolonged analysis.

bands.label: average
bands.text:
You're capable of analytical thinking and research when a task calls for it, without an excessive love of pure theory.

This gives you a practical balance between analysis and action.

bands.label: high
bands.text:
You're drawn to solving complex, abstract problems through analysis, research, and systematic thinking.

This is a valuable trait for scientific, research, and analytical professions where depth of understanding matters before action.

questions.text: I enjoy analyzing complex problems.
I like figuring out why something works the way it does.
Research and experiments appeal to me.
I prefer deep analysis over a quick solution.

bands.label: low
bands.text:
Creative self-expression and unconventional, unstructured forms of activity aren't your main interest.

You likely feel better in structured, predictable work formats.

bands.label: average
bands.text:
You value creativity to a moderate degree, pairing it with more structured approaches to work.

This gives you flexibility between creativity and practical execution.

bands.label: high
bands.text:
Creative self-expression, originality, and aesthetics matter to you. You seek unconventional solutions and dislike rigid constraints.

This is a valuable trait for creative, design, and artistic professions, where originality is valued over conformity.

questions.text: Creative self-expression matters to me.
I enjoy coming up with unconventional solutions.
Aesthetics and beauty matter to me.
I prefer freedom of expression over rigid constraints.

bands.label: low
bands.text:
Work centered on directly helping or teaching others isn't your main professional interest.

You're likely more effective in roles that call for independent or technical work.

bands.label: average
bands.text:
You're capable of, and enjoy, occasionally helping or teaching others, combining this with other kinds of activity.

This gives you a balance between social interaction and individual work.

bands.label: high
bands.text:
Helping, teaching, and caring about others' development matter to you professionally. You naturally gravitate toward roles where you can have an impact on people's wellbeing.

This is a valuable trait for teaching, counseling, and social professions.

questions.text: I enjoy helping other people.
I get satisfaction from teaching or mentoring others.
Caring about people's wellbeing matters to me.
I enjoy working as a team toward a shared goal.

bands.label: low
bands.text:
Leadership, persuasion, and competitive business activity aren't your main interest.

You likely feel better in roles that don't require constantly persuading or managing others.

bands.label: average
bands.text:
You're capable of taking on a leadership role or negotiating when necessary, without constantly seeking it out.

This gives you flexibility between leading and executing.

bands.label: high
bands.text:
Leadership, persuasion, negotiation, and pursuing ambitious goals naturally appeal to you.

This is a valuable trait for management, entrepreneurial, and sales professions.

questions.text: I enjoy leading others.
I easily persuade people of my point of view.
Achieving ambitious goals motivates me.
I'm comfortable negotiating.

bands.label: low
bands.text:
Structured, detail-oriented work with data or documents isn't your main interest.

You likely feel better in roles with more freedom and fewer fixed procedures.

bands.label: average
bands.text:
You're capable of working with clear procedures and data when necessary, combining this with other kinds of activity.

This gives you a balance between structure and flexibility.

bands.label: high
bands.text:
Precision, order, and clear procedures matter to you. You feel comfortable working with data, documents, and structured systems.

This is a valuable trait for administrative, financial, and operational professions.

questions.text: I enjoy working within clear structures and procedures.
Precision and order matter to me at work.
I prefer clear instructions over ambiguity.
Working with data or documents doesn't bother me.

portraits.title: The Investigator-Builder
portraits.text: You combine a love of hands-on, tangible work with an analytical approach to solving problems. This combination is typical of engineering, technical, and applied science professions.

portraits.title: The Creative Mentor
portraits.text: Creative self-expression is paired with a desire to help others. This combination is common among art therapists, arts educators, and people who teach through creative work.

portraits.title: The Strategic Leader
portraits.text: Analytical thinking is paired with entrepreneurial energy. This combination is typical of consulting, strategic management, and data-driven business development.

portraits.title: The Systematic Helper
portraits.text: A drive for order is paired with a desire to help people. This combination is common among social-services administrators, education-program coordinators, and HR professionals.

portraits.title: The Visionary Maker
portraits.text: Creative vision is paired with a practical ability to turn ideas into material form. This combination is typical of product designers, architects, and craftspeople with an artistic bent.
